visa_types.name: Cultural/sport Events/study
visa_types.documents_required:
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
Please click here for the documentation checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7srKbj0Y33zg36Z8pCdXVu/010002dc0e455e98cdd8d3b5c1853b29/checklist_document_crotia_visa.pdf)
Please click here for the Embassy’s checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1cfH69R8pOLuyKjGtTEoOl/d6b6f2b15c9470ad8bd9253bf4cbf9f7/checklist_for_cultural_study_sports.pdf)
The documents attached shall not be older than three months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification (appostille) of the documents during the visa application process.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[hrv > mmr > en > Table:Cultural/sport Events/study > Visa Fees 1]
The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children up to the age of 12,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional trainings, in Croatia
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change periodically.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- All service charges are to be paid in cash only in USD (VFS service charge and other charges (http://liftpoc.vfsglobal.com.s3-website.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/mmr/en/hrv/additional-services))
visa_types.processing_time:
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa processing time is up to 10 days. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview: https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879 (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)
visa_types.forms:
Applicants who wish to apply for a visa to Croatia must use the Online Visa Application Form available at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en). The Online Form is available in Croatian, English, Albanian, Turkish, Russian and Ukrainian language. Once you have successfully completed the Online Application Form by filling out data and uploading mandatory documents; photo, passport page, flight ticket, and itinerary. You are requested to print a copy and submit it along with the Documents Required (refer to 
https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/)) to the Croatian Visa Application Centre in Yangon, Myanmar.
Please click herefor Instruction of Online Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2j7mtHytW0AI7cgo6BqKO5/ccff6ade9f2198daae8635728741cc41/online-application-form.pdf)  (Serial number and PIN need to be provided for checking.)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is issued to applicants invited to participate in Sport competitions (both professionals and amateurs), Cultural events and Study courses in Croatia only for a short term period of time, maximum 90 days.
The original invitation letter must be submitted, by the Croatian inviting organizations, at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Croatia and a copy of this document must be sent to the applicant requesting the Cultural/Sport Events/Study Visa.
Click here to access the “Letter of Guarantee by Legal Person (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/forms-22819/22819)”
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is issued to applicants invited to participate in Sport competitions (both professionals and amateurs), Cultural events and Study courses in Croatia only for a short term period of time, maximum 90 days.
The original invitation letter must be submitted, by the Croatian inviting organizations, at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Croatia and a copy of this document must be sent to the applicant requesting the Cultural/Sport Events/Study Visa.
Click here to access the “Letter of Guarantee by Legal Person (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/forms-22819/22819)”
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.

## Visa Fees

[hrv > mmr > en > Table:Cultural/sport Events/study > Visa Fees 1]
The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children up to the age of 12,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional trainings, in Croatia
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change periodically.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- All service charges are to be paid in cash only in USD (VFS service charge and other charges (http://liftpoc.vfsglobal.com.s3-website.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/mmr/en/hrv/additional-services))

## Documents Required
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
Please click here for the documentation checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7srKbj0Y33zg36Z8pCdXVu/010002dc0e455e98cdd8d3b5c1853b29/checklist_document_crotia_visa.pdf)
Please click here for the Embassy’s checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1cfH69R8pOLuyKjGtTEoOl/d6b6f2b15c9470ad8bd9253bf4cbf9f7/checklist_for_cultural_study_sports.pdf)
The documents attached shall not be older than three months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification (appostille) of the documents during the visa application process.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here to check the photo specifications (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6HGxw5GG4ZOmMvULGsaqR6/5d8638e9f4d39525ee9c6c2afc04091b/Tourist.pdf)
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the presented photos do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa processing time is up to 10 days. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview: https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879 (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

## Download Forms
Applicants who wish to apply for a visa to Croatia must use the Online Visa Application Form available at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en). The Online Form is available in Croatian, English, Albanian, Turkish, Russian and Ukrainian language. Once you have successfully completed the Online Application Form by filling out data and uploading mandatory documents; photo, passport page, flight ticket, and itinerary. You are requested to print a copy and submit it along with the Documents Required (refer to 
https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/)) to the Croatian Visa Application Centre in Yangon, Myanmar.
Please click herefor Instruction of Online Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2j7mtHytW0AI7cgo6BqKO5/ccff6ade9f2198daae8635728741cc41/online-application-form.pdf)  (Serial number and PIN need to be provided for checking.)

visa_types.name: Family Or Friends Visit
visa_types.documents_required:
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
Please click here for the documentation checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7srKbj0Y33zg36Z8pCdXVu/010002dc0e455e98cdd8d3b5c1853b29/checklist_document_crotia_visa.pdf)
Please click here for the Embassy’s checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2w4mHd31P7xAqPs4A6tLR0/bdfac6b8674b1124b7338fe4344ff8e9/checklist_visiting_family_and_friends.pdf)
The documents attached shall not be older than three months.
If a minor is traveling without a parent/legal guardian, (s)he must carry a notarized consent of a legal guardian that shall contain personal information of the parent/legal guardian and the purpose, the period and length of stay in Croatia, the period for which the consent is issued, and the signature of the parent/legal guardian.
The documents attached shall not be older than three months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification (appostille) of the documents during the visa application process.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This kind of Visa applies to those who are invited to visit family, friends in Croatia.
The original invitation letter must be submitted, by the Croatian inviting person, at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Croatia and a copy of this document must be sent to the applicant requesting the Visiting Visa
Click here to access the “Letter of Guarantee by Natural Person (http://www.mvep.hr/files/file/obrasci/JamstvenoPismoFizickaOsoba.pdf)”
(Maximum duration of stay: 90 days)
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.

visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/hrv/travel-insurance)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The airport transit visa allows a foreign national subject to the obligation to enter the international transit area of airports without actually entering the territory of the Contracting Party which issued the visa.
(Maximum duration of stay: 24 hours, Applicant cannot leave the airport)
This kind of Visa is also granted to Seamen who need to embark a ship docked in a Croatian Port.
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The airport transit visa allows a foreign national subject to the obligation to enter the international transit area of airports without actually entering the territory of the Contracting Party which issued the visa.
(Maximum duration of stay: 24 hours, Applicant cannot leave the airport)
This kind of Visa is also granted to Seamen who need to embark a ship docked in a Croatian Port.
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.

However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa processing time is up to 10 days. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview: https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879 (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

## Download Forms
Applicants who wish to apply for a visa to Croatia must use the Online Visa Application Form available at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en). The Online Form is available in Croatian, English, Albanian, Turkish, Russian and Ukrainian language. Once you have successfully completed the Online Application Form by filling out data and uploading mandatory documents; photo, passport page, flight ticket, and itinerary. You are requested to print a copy and submit it along with the Documents Required (refer to 
https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/)) to the Croatian Visa Application Centre in Yangon, Myanmar.
Please click herefor Instruction of Online Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2j7mtHytW0AI7cgo6BqKO5/ccff6ade9f2198daae8635728741cc41/online-application-form.pdf)  (Serial number and PIN need to be provided for checking.)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. This visa is given for a travel period maximum of 90 days.
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. This visa is given for a travel period maximum of 90 days.
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.

## Visa Fees

[hrv > mmr > en > Table:Tourist > Visa Fees 1]
- The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children up to the age of 12,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional trainings, in Croatia
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change periodically.
- All fees are non-refundable.
All service charges are to be paid in cash only in USD (VFS service charge and other charges (http://liftpoc.vfsglobal.com.s3-website.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/mmr/en/hrv/additional-services))

## Documents Required
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
Please click here for the Embassy’s checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3BGO8RSBa19elae7fEkCAq/ae5c861b55c21a737e7df3848bd4c41a/checklist-for-tourist-application.pdf):
If a minor is traveling without a parent/legal guardian, (s)he must carry a notarized consent of a legal guardian that shall contain personal information of the parent/legal guardian and the purpose, the period and length of stay in Croatia, the period for which the consent is issued, and the signature of the parent/legal guardian.
The documents attached shall not be older than three months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification (appostille) of the documents during the visa application process.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here to check the photo specifications (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6HGxw5GG4ZOmMvULGsaqR6/5d8638e9f4d39525ee9c6c2afc04091b/Tourist.pdf)
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the presented photos do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa processing time is up to 10 days. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview: https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879 (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

## Download Forms
Applicants who wish to apply for a visa to Croatia must use the Online Visa Application Form available at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en). The Online Form is available in Croatian, English, Albanian, Turkish, Russian and Ukrainian language. Once you have successfully completed the Online Application Form by filling out data and uploading mandatory documents; photo, passport page, flight ticket, and itinerary. You are requested to print a copy and submit it along with the Documents Required (refer to 
https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/)) to the Croatian Visa Application Centre in Yangon, Myanmar.
Please click herefor Instruction of Online Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2j7mtHytW0AI7cgo6BqKO5/ccff6ade9f2198daae8635728741cc41/online-application-form.pdf)  (Serial number and PIN need to be provided for checking.)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A business visa is granted for those who are invited by a Croatian company / business partner to visit Croatia for business purposes for a short period of stay (not more than 90 days).
The original invitation letter must be submitted, by the Croatian inviting company, at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Croatia and a copy of this document must be sent to the applicant requesting the Business Visa.
Click here to access the “Letter of Guarantee by Legal Person (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/forms-22819/22819)”
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A business visa is granted for those who are invited by a Croatian company / business partner to visit Croatia for business purposes for a short period of stay (not more than 90 days).
The original invitation letter must be submitted, by the Croatian inviting company, at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Croatia and a copy of this document must be sent to the applicant requesting the Business Visa.
Click here to access the “Letter of Guarantee by Legal Person (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/forms-22819/22819)”
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.

## Visa Fees

[hrv > mmr > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Official Invitation Visa is granted for those where the intention is to visit Croatia for official purposes. Applicants falling under this category are the members of official delegations invited to participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programs.
The original invitation letter must be submitted, by the Croatian inviting organizations, at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Croatia and a copy of this document must be sent to the applicant requesting the Official Visa.
Click here to access the “Letter of Guarantee by Legal Person (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/forms-22819/22819)”
(Maximum duration of stay: 90 days)
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.

Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification (appostille) of the documents during the visa application process.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here to check the photo specifications (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6HGxw5GG4ZOmMvULGsaqR6/5d8638e9f4d39525ee9c6c2afc04091b/Tourist.pdf)
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the presented photos do not follow these requirements your application will be considered incomplete!

## Processing Time
Standard visa processing time is up to 15 days from the receipt of the application at the Embassy (it could be shorter, depending on the documentation). However, in case the documents are not submitted as per requirement or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended to 30 or 60 days.
For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa processing time is up to 10 days. For further details please consult the Visa Facilitation Agreements available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs under Consular Information – Visas- Visa Requirements Overview: https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879 (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/visa-requirements-overview-22879/22879)

## Download Forms
Applicants who wish to apply for a visa to Croatia must use the Online Visa Application Form available at https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/?lang=en). The Online Form is available in Croatian, English, Albanian, Turkish, Russian and Ukrainian language. Once you have successfully completed the Online Application Form by filling out data and uploading mandatory documents; photo, passport page, flight ticket, and itinerary. You are requested to print a copy and submit it along with the Documents Required (refer to 
https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/croatia/myanmar/english/)) to the Croatian Visa Application Centre in Yangon, Myanmar.
Please click herefor Instruction of Online Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2j7mtHytW0AI7cgo6BqKO5/ccff6ade9f2198daae8635728741cc41/online-application-form.pdf)  (Serial number and PIN need to be provided for checking.)

visa_types.name: Medical Treatment
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/hrv/travel-insurance)
The applicants are requested to read the basic requirements carefully and conform to the same while submitting the application.
Please click here for the documentation checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7srKbj0Y33zg36Z8pCdXVu/010002dc0e455e98cdd8d3b5c1853b29/checklist_document_crotia_visa.pdf)
Please click here for the Embassy’s checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/406uvAFd4rlda8FH3ZGpCt/8f07d9207e8edb37f3e8cd3bbc54a1dc/checklist_for_medical_treatment.pdf)
If a minor is traveling without a parent/legal guardian, (s)he must carry a notarized consent of a legal guardian that shall contain personal information of the parent/legal guardian and the purpose, the period and length of stay in Croatia, the period for which the consent is issued, and the signature of the parent/legal guardian.
The documents attached shall not be older than three months.
Croatian Embassy may require you to submit additional supporting documents and/or certified translation as well as the international certification (appostille) of the documents during the visa application process.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[hrv > mmr > en > Table:Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children up to the age of 12,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional trainings, in Croatia
- Researchers coming to conduct research, as provided for by the Recommendation of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research,
- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change periodically.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- All service charges are to be paid in cash only in USD (VFS service charge and other charges (http://liftpoc.vfsglobal.com.s3-website.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/mmr/en/hrv/additional-services))

For citizens of Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia, Moldova (holders of non-biometric passports), Ukraine and Russian Federation, visa processing time is up to 10 days. visa_types.overview:
Overview
This kind of Visa applies to those who are intending to receive medical treatments in Croatia.
(Maximum duration of stay: 90 days)
The original invitation letter must be submitted, by the Croatian admitting hospitalization, at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Croatia and a copy of this document must be sent to the applicant requesting the Medical Treatment Visa.
Click here to access the “Letter of Guarantee by Legal Person (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/forms-22819/22819)”
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This kind of Visa applies to those who are intending to receive medical treatments in Croatia.
(Maximum duration of stay: 90 days)
The original invitation letter must be submitted, by the Croatian admitting hospitalization, at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Croatia and a copy of this document must be sent to the applicant requesting the Medical Treatment Visa.
Click here to access the “Letter of Guarantee by Legal Person (https://mvep.gov.hr/services-for-citizens/consular-information-22802/visas-22807/forms-22819/22819)”
Visa applications shall be submitted no sooner than 90 days prior to the date of the intended trip. It is advisable to lodge the Visa application at least 15 working days before the intended visit.

## Visa Fees

[hrv > mmr > en > Table:Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
The following are exempt from visa fees upon application:
- Family members of a Croatian citizen (spouse and children),
- Family members of citizens of EEA Member States,
- Children up to the age of 12,
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports travelling in their official capacity
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and teachers accompanying them when they come to attend school, study and professional trainings, in Croatia

- Representatives of non-profit organizations up to the age of 25, who are to attend seminars, conferences, sport, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
Please Note:
- The applicable fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change periodically.
- All fees are non-refundable.
